---
title: (Reduced) Row Echelon Form
description: The REF and RREF conditions, uniqueness of the RREF, and a worked reduction.
categories:
- Linear Algebra
---

**Row echelon form (REF):**

1. All zero rows are below all non-zero rows.
2. The leading entry of each non-zero row is strictly to the right of the leading entry of the row above.

**Reduced row echelon form (RREF):** REF, plus

3. every leading entry is $1$, and
4. each leading $1$ is the only non-zero entry in its column.

**Theorems.** Every matrix can be put in RREF using finitely many elementary row operations, and the RREF of a matrix is **unique**.

**Example.**

$$
\left(\begin{array}{cc|c} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right) \xrightarrow{R_2 \to \frac12 R_2} \left(\begin{array}{cc|c} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & \frac12 \end{array}\right) \xrightarrow{R_1 \to R_1 - R_2} \left(\begin{array}{cc|c} 1 & 0 & \frac12 \\ 0 & 1 & \frac12 \end{array}\right)
$$
